steps.thought: I'll start by observing the initial state. I'll view the current frame. A residential street heading toward a stop-sign intersection. Let me open the map to locate the goal relative to my position.

The map is clear. The GOAL (red pin) is roughly north of my position (blue YOU pin at bottom), about 119m up along Southwest 122nd Avenue. North is up on the map. I'm currently facing west (heading 269°), so I need to turn right toward north and walk up the road.

Now in pano view facing west (269°). The goal is north, so I need to rotate my view clockwise (right) toward north. I'll test a click-drag to rotate. Let me press down at center, drag left (which rotates the camera right), and release.

Dragging left rotated me clockwise: heading went 268.8° → 288.8° (+20° for 256px). I need to reach ~358° (north), so ~69° more. Let me continue dragging while the mouse is down (cursor is now near x=256, can drag further left) — actually let me release and use a larger drag from center.

Now facing 289° (WNW). I can see the road ahead leads to the stop-sign intersection — that's the 122nd Ave × 191st Terrace corner. Per the map, the goal is north up 122nd Ave. So I'll walk west to the intersection first, then turn north.
Cursor is at (330, 430), on the road surface below the horizon, leading toward the intersection. Let me click (mouse_down + mouse_up) to walk there.

Moved forward — pano changed and distance dropped 118.8m → 108.2m. Let me view the new position. I moved west and got ~10m closer, but I should re-localize. Let me re-open the map to see my updated position vs the goal.

Now clearer. YOU (blue, arrow pointing west) at the 191st Terrace/122nd Ave area; GOAL (red) is north and very slightly east, up Southwest 122nd Avenue (~one block north). I'm just at/past the 122nd Ave intersection. I need to turn north and walk up 122nd Ave.
Now I need to rotate from 279° to ~360° (north), about +81°. Dragging left increases heading (~20° per 256px). Let me first move the cursor to the right edge to have room to drag left.
Heading now 348° (NNW). Let me release and view the pano to find the northbound road.

Facing 348° (NNW). I see the pink corner house at the NW corner of 122nd Ave & 191st Terrace, and the northbound road (122nd Ave, with street sign) is to my right (east). I overshot slightly west. I need to rotate right (~+35°) to face the northbound 122nd Ave road.

Now facing 24° (NNE). Let me view to locate the northbound road. Now facing 24° (NNE) — perfect. I can see 122nd Ave heading north (double-yellow road, slightly left of center, with a car on it). I'll click on that road surface to walk north.
Cursor at (425, 420) on the northbound road. Let me click to walk north.
Big jump — distance dropped 108m → 64.6m, now facing 360° (due north). Let me view. On 122nd Ave facing due north (360°), 64.6m to goal — straight ahead. Let me click up the road to continue north.
Distance dropped to 16.1m! Very close. Let me view and check the map for precise positioning. 16m from goal, facing north. Let me open the map to position precisely for the final approach.

YOU (blue) and GOAL (red) are nearly overlapping — goal is ~16m directly north. One small step north should land me on it. Let me close the map and take a short step (clicking low on the road for a small move).
dist_to_goal = 0.0m — I'm right on the goal! Let me submit my guess.

The first westward move changed pano and slightly reduced distance, so it is a connected route but not strongly toward the goal. I’ll continue west briefly, then re-check map/pano for a southbound option.
The second westward move increased distance slightly, so I’ve likely passed the best southbound line. I’m checking the map again from the new position to choose the turn.

The map confirms the goal is just south of the block, and the current spot is west of the original start on NE 15th. I need either a south passage from here or a return east to Biscayne and then south.

There is a visible left-side paved opening under the rail, which corresponds to the south side of the frame while facing west. I’m going to try that connection because it points toward the goal more directly than continuing west.

That click advanced to another westbound pano and moved farther from the goal, so the apparent opening was not the needed route. I’m going to rotate the camera to look east/south rather than keep walking west.
Dragging right reduced the view yaw from about 269 to 245 degrees, so it rotates the camera leftward. I’ll continue that direction to face south.
The cursor hit the screen edge with the camera around southwest. I’ll release, recenter if needed, and inspect what road options are visible.

The southwest view shows a fenced/private area, not a street connection. I’m rotating farther left toward east so I can backtrack to Biscayne Boulevard, where the map shows a reliable southbound route.
I’ve recentered the cursor for another pan. Dragging right will keep reducing the compass heading toward east.
The heading is now southeast/south-southeast, still short of east. I’ll pan once more toward east and then walk back along NE 15th toward Biscayne.
Continuing the controlled pano drag; no walking is happening during these drag movements.
This click target is on the visible road surface in the eastbound direction.

The click moved back one pano and the camera now faces east, which is the desired direction to Biscayne. I’ll keep walking east until near the boulevard, then turn south. The cursor is currently over a parked car, so I’ll retarget to the center of the drivable lane before clicking east.
That moved east two panos and improved the distance slightly. I’m continuing east toward Biscayne before turning south.
The lane target remains valid, so this next click should continue east along NE 15th.

The last eastward step started increasing distance again, which suggests the best turn south may be near this segment rather than farther east. I’ll use the map to confirm the position and choose the south turn.

The map clarifies the target: the current position is almost directly north of the goal, separated by the block between NE 15th and NE 14th. I need to face south and look for the internal driveway/sidewalk path shown through the District Instructional Center block.
Back in pano, facing east. I’ll rotate right toward south to inspect the block-side access.
The heading is moving from east toward southeast; I’ll continue the same drag until south is centered.
The cursor hit the left edge around southeast. I’ll release, recenter the cursor, and continue panning to south.
Cursor is recentered for another controlled pan.
Continuing the pan toward due south.

The heading is near south now, so I’ll inspect the frame for a legitimate walkable path through the block. The direct south view is blocked by buildings and sidewalk, so the map’s straight-line route is not traversable from here.
I’m rotating back toward east to follow NE 15th to the boulevard.
The camera is back near east. I’ll release the drag and walk east to the boulevard.
The cursor ended at the screen edge after panning, so I’m moving it back onto the center of the lane before the eastbound click.
Clicking the lane center to continue east.
Now back at the starting pano, facing east toward Biscayne. I’ll continue to the intersection.
The eastbound click moved close to Biscayne and increased straight-line distance, which is expected for the grid route. I’ll confirm the intersection position before turning south.

The map frame will show whether I’m at the turn onto Biscayne or still on NE 15th. I’m just west of Biscayne; one more short eastward move should put me at the boulevard connection, then I can turn south.
Back in pano facing east, I’ll make a short lane click toward the boulevard.
That moved to the boulevard side and increased straight-line distance, so the next productive move is south. I’ll check the map once more to orient the turn precisely.
The blue pin is at the Biscayne edge of NE 15th. The goal is south and west from here, so I’m turning right onto the southbound boulevard.
Back in pano; I’ll pan from east toward south.
Heading is southeast; continuing the pan to south.
The cursor reached the left edge again; I’ll release and repeat a shorter recentered drag to finish the southward turn.
Cursor recentered; continuing toward south.

The camera is near south. I’ll inspect the boulevard view and then click down the lane if visible. The southbound boulevard is visible ahead, with NE 15th behind the intersection line. I’ll walk south along Biscayne toward NE 14th.
Clicking the road just beyond the intersection.
Now on the southbound boulevard and distance dropped by about 10 m. I’ll continue south toward NE 14th Street.
The lane target remains centered southbound.
Another southbound move improved distance to about 126 m. Continuing down Biscayne.
Clicking the road ahead again.
Distance is down to about 111 m, still north/east of the goal. I’ll keep moving south until I reach the NE 14th Street turn.
Still improving; continuing south on Biscayne.
Clicking forward toward the NE 14th area.
Distance is about 88 m, close to the expected westward offset from Biscayne to the goal. I’ll check the map now so I don’t overshoot the NE 14th turn.
The map shows I’m still north of NE 14th, so I should not turn west yet. I’ll continue south on Biscayne until level with the red pin, then turn west.
Back in pano already facing south; moving farther down Biscayne.
Distance is now about 76 m, which is close to the remaining westward offset. I’ll check the map before deciding whether to turn west or go one more southbound step.

Map check at this southbound position. I’ll inspect the map frame and then turn west if the blue pin is level with NE 14th. The blue pin is still north of the goal and on Biscayne. I need one more southbound step before turning west onto NE 14th.
Continuing south one more step.
Clicking the boulevard lane ahead.
The southbound move improved distance to about 64 m. I’ll map-check; this may be the point to turn west.
The blue pin is still just above NE 14th, so I need one more south step to reach the intersection before the west turn.
Continuing south to line up with NE 14th.
Clicking southbound once more.
Distance is now about 55 m. I’m checking map before turning west; this should be near the NE 14th intersection.

I’ll inspect this map frame to confirm west-turn alignment. The blue pin is still slightly north of the NE 14th line, so one final southbound step should put me at or just past the intersection. After that I’ll turn west.
Continuing south to the NE 14th turn point.
Clicking ahead on the boulevard.
Distance dropped to about 50 m; now I should be at the west turn zone. I’ll map-check once more and then head west.

Inspecting the map; if the blue pin is just east of the red pin, I’ll turn west onto NE 14th. The blue pin is now level with the goal on NE 14th, just east of it. I’m turning west and will move along NE 14th toward the red pin.
Back in pano facing south, rotating right toward west.
Dragging left increases heading toward west.
Heading is southwest; continuing toward west.
The cursor hit the edge around southwest; I’ll recenter and finish turning to west.
Repositioning the cursor for the remaining pan.
Continuing the turn to west.
Heading is now just south of west; that should be good enough to see NE 14th westbound. I’ll release and inspect the road alignment.
NE 14th westbound is visible. I’ll move the cursor onto the road center and walk west toward the goal.
Clicking west along NE 14th.
Releasing on the road target.
The westbound move improved distance to about 39 m. I’ll continue west, likely one or two more panos to reach the pin.
Road target remains centered westbound.
Clicking forward west.
Distance is down to about 24 m. I’ll check the map to avoid walking past the red pin.

Map check at 24 m remaining. I’ll inspect the map frame and decide whether to submit or make one final westward adjustment. The blue pin is east of the red pin on the same street, about 24 m away. I’ll make one more westward move, aiming short and centered, then reassess.
Back to the westbound pano; I’ll use a shorter road click.
The cursor is now lower on the road for a shorter step.
Releasing on the nearer westbound road point.
The short westward move improved distance to about 16 m. I’ll check the map before deciding whether to submit or make one final adjustment.
Map check at 16 m remaining. The blue pin is still a little east of the red pin on the same street. I’ll take one more very short west step, then submit unless it clearly overshoots.
Using the existing low road target for a short westward step.
Clicking the short westward target.
I'm on a residential street. Let me open the map to see the goal direction.

The map shows me (blue "YOU" pin, center) and the GOAL (red pin) to the east-southeast along South Main Street. My current heading (145° SE) roughly faces down the road. Let me close the map and walk along the road toward the goal.
Now I'll walk forward along the road. Let me position the cursor on the road surface ahead and click.
Distance dropped from 327m to 281m. Let me view the new frame. Road continues straight (SE, 131°). Cursor is on the road. Let me click to walk forward again.
237m now. Let me view and continue. Road continues straight SE (125°). Let me nudge the cursor down onto clear road (avoiding the car ahead) and click.
209m. Continuing. Road continues straight SE. Cursor is on the road ahead. Clicking to advance.
186m. Let me view. I'm approaching a junction. Let me check the map to confirm the route before choosing a road.

Good — YOU (blue) is now well along South Main Street; GOAL (red) is just ~145px east, slightly south. I need to keep following South Main Street eastward. Let me close the map and continue on the main road (the one on the right in the pano).
The main road (South Main St) is on the right, heading east. The cursor is on the median — I need to move it right onto the road surface. Let me move the cursor right onto the road and click.
Cursor is now on the road surface to the right. Clicking to walk along the road.
155m. Let me view. Road continues straight toward a traffic light ahead (125° SE). Cursor is off on the right shoulder — let me move it back to the center road and click.
Cursor is on the road. Clicking to walk forward.
134m. Let me view. Approaching the traffic-light intersection. Road continues straight, cursor on road. Clicking forward.
118m. Let me view. At the intersection, road continues straight. Cursor on road ahead. Clicking forward.
108m. Getting close — let me check the map to fine-tune direction.
YOU (blue) is now southwest of GOAL (red), which sits at the corner near Halls Hill Road — goal is now roughly northeast of me. Let me zoom in to see the road connectivity precisely.
The map re-centered on START. Let me zoom back out one notch to see YOU and GOAL together with the connecting road.

YOU (blue) is now southwest of GOAL — the goal bears roughly NE (~46°), but I'm heading SE (124°). I've drifted a bit south. The goal sits at South Main St × Halls Hill Road. Let me close the map and look at the pano to find the road toward the goal (to my left/east).
I'm on South Main Street (124° SE), distance still decreasing each step. The road continues ahead. Let me step forward and monitor whether distance keeps dropping. Cursor is on the road.
104m. Small step — I may be nearing closest approach. Let me view. Road continues SE. Let me take a larger step by clicking further down the road toward the vanishing point.

Distance jumped up to 132m — I overshot the closest approach (was 104m at pano i8). The goal is off to the side, not straight ahead. Let me check the map to understand the road layout and where the goal connects.

YOU (blue) is now directly south of GOAL (~103m north) — I overshot on South Main Street. The goal is offset ~100m NE of this street segment, likely up Halls Hill Road which branches off near the closest point. Let me close the map, go back toward the closest approach, and look for the branch road.

I'm at i0 (overshot, McDonald's on left). The goal is now behind-left (NE). There's a side road branching right (SW, wrong way). I need to survey for a branch heading NE toward the goal, and likely backtrack. Let me pan the camera left (drag right) to look toward the goal direction (NE).

Camera rotated to face ~90° (E). Let me view to see what's toward the goal direction. Facing E now — McDonald's is on the north side, goal is NE (to my left). Let me pan further left to face the goal direction (NE ~46°) and look for an access road.
The drag started from the screen edge so it didn't rotate. Let me reposition the cursor to the left side first, then drag right to rotate the view left.

Now facing NE (42°), toward the goal. Let me view. Facing NE there's only the John Deere dealership and McDonald's — no road toward the goal from i0. The goal is beyond these buildings. The Halls Hill Road junction must be elsewhere.
Goal is off-screen SE. Let me pan the map toward the goal by dragging the view.

Now I can see the goal clearly: GOAL (red) is at "Children's Dental Associates" on Halls Hill Road, directly NORTH of YOU (~132m). South Main Street runs NW-SE through YOU; Halls Hill Road runs along the top by the goal. I need to find where Halls Hill Road connects to a road I can walk.

Pan persisted. I can see South Main Street with traffic-light intersections, and Halls Hill Road branches NE from a junction up toward the goal. The goal is just north (off-screen top). Let me pan down to see the Halls Hill Road / South Main junction and the goal together.

The GOAL is on Halls Hill Road, which branches off South Main Street near Gibbs (NW of my current position, around my earlier closest-approach point). There's a street-view path along Halls Hill Road.

The HUD shows I'm still facing NE (042°); South Main Street runs left-right across the bottom of the view. To walk back NW toward the Halls Hill junction, let me rotate the camera left to face NW. I'll drag right from the left edge.
Now facing NNW (328°) — South Main Street recedes ahead-left, the way back toward Gibbs/the Halls Hill junction. Let me position the cursor on the road and walk back NW.
Cursor is on the road heading NW. Clicking to walk back toward the junction.

Back to 103.5m at pano i10 (near closest approach). Now I need to find the Halls Hill Road junction to turn NE toward the goal. Let me view. I'm at an intersection with a traffic light and Citgo gas station (the junction near Gibbs). The goal is NE (to my right). There's a cross street here.

The map is panned to the goal area; YOU is just off the bottom-left near the Gibbs/CT85 junction. Halls Hill Road (with a street-view path) runs from that junction NE up to the GOAL. Let me pan the map up to see my position and the exact junction.

Excellent — now it's clear. YOU (blue) is at a traffic-light intersection on South Main Street, with Citgo just NE and Halls Hill Road running roughly north of me toward the GOAL. I need to head NE/N onto the Halls Hill Road corridor.
Now facing NNE (24°), toward the goal. Let me view. Facing NE there's only the Citgo station — no road. The goal is beyond it. The Halls Hill Road junction must be reached via the road network.

I can now trace it: Halls Hill Road (white) branches off South Main Street at a junction ~75m NW of me (at a traffic light), then runs NE to the GOAL. The plaza directly NE has no through-road. So the route is: NW on South Main to the Halls Hill junction → turn NE up Halls Hill Road to the goal.
Facing NW (310°) toward a traffic-light intersection ahead — likely the Halls Hill Road junction. Citgo is on my right. Let me advance to the intersection. I'll position the cursor on the road ahead.
Cursor is on the road toward the intersection. Clicking to advance to the junction.

No movement (still i10) — the click may have hit a vehicle. Let me view and retry on clear road. The road ahead (NW) leads to the traffic light. Let me place the cursor clearly on the road surface and walk.
Cursor is on the road now. Clicking to walk NW to the intersection.
Still no movement — the click point may be too far/near the horizon. Let me check the cursor and click closer on the road. Let me click closer to me on the road (lower on screen).

I moved onto a new street segment (w17159322) — distance 117m. Let me view to see where I am. This turned onto a residential side street going SW — away from the goal (distance rose to 117m). This isn't Halls Hill Road. Let me check the map to reorient.

I'm essentially at the intersection now, but on the SW side-street arm (facing SW, away from goal). I need to turn ~180° to face NE toward Halls Hill Road and the goal. Let me close the map and rotate.
Now at SE (149°). One more rotation to reach NE.

I'm at the main intersection (Citgo on the NE corner, traffic lights, crosswalks). Halls Hill Road should branch NE from here toward the goal, but the Citgo plaza occupies that corner. Let me examine the map at this intersection carefully to find exactly where Halls Hill Road's path connects.

Now it's completely clear! YOU are right at the junction where Halls Hill Road branches off — and Halls Hill Road (with a street-view path) runs NE directly to the GOAL. The goal bears ~60° (ENE), and I'm already facing 69°.

I'm at the intersection facing ENE (69°), goal bearing ~60° (just left of center). Halls Hill Road heads ENE past the Citgo toward the goal. Let me position the cursor on the road heading that way and walk.
Cursor is on the road heading E/ENE (toward Halls Hill Road / the goal). Let me click and check the distance change.

Distance dropped to 100m at pano i2 — I'm now on the right road (Halls Hill direction) heading toward the goal. Let me view and continue. On Halls Hill Road now (bike lane visible), heading ENE toward the goal (100m). Let me walk forward along the road.
Cursor on the road ahead. Walking forward toward the goal.
68.5m now (pano i6)! Making great progress up Halls Hill Road. Let me view and continue. Road continues straight ENE, goal 68m ahead. Cursor on the road. Walking forward.

30.2m now (pano i11) — very close! Let me view to find the exact goal location. Very close (30m) on Halls Hill Road — there's a building with a sign on the left (likely Children's Dental). Let me check the map to pinpoint the goal so I don't overshoot.

YOU is on Halls Hill Road, GOAL just ~30m further ENE along the same road (the path runs right through it). One more careful step should get me very close. Let me close the map and take a controlled step.
Goal is ~30m straight ahead. To avoid overshooting, I'll take a controlled shorter step by clicking a bit closer on the road.

11.4m now (pano i13)! Almost there. Let me view and take one more small step. I'm 11.4m away — the Children's Dental Associates building (the goal) is right on my left. Let me check the map to see if stepping forward gets me closer or if I'm at the closest point.
YOU is just ~11m short of the GOAL, which is a touch further ENE on Halls Hill Road. One more small step forward should put me right on it. Let me close the map and step.
One small step forward to reach the goal. Clicking close on the road for a minimal step.
Distance is now 0.0m — I'm exactly at the goal (pano i15)! Let me submit my guess.
I'll start by observing the initial state. I'm on a street in Miami facing west (~269°), with an elevated rail on the left. Let me open the map to locate the goal.

I can see the map. YOU (blue pin) are on NE 15th Street; the GOAL (red pin) is south and slightly west, on NE 14th Street — about one block+ south. Let me zoom in to understand the street connections.
YOU are on NE 15th Street near Biscayne Boulevard; the goal scrolled off-screen to the south. Let me zoom back out one notch to frame both pins and plan the full route.

YOU and GOAL are nearly the same longitude — the goal is essentially due south, about 1.4 blocks down (NE 15th St → NE 14th St). The yellow "compass" rose earlier and heading 269° confirm I'm facing west. I need to find a southbound road.
I'm facing west. I need to rotate to find a southbound road toward the goal. Let me test the drag-to-pan direction with one drag and read the resulting yaw.

Now facing heading 190.8° (nearly south). Let me view the pano. South is blocked — that's the school building (CCE Miami / theater) across NE 15th St; no road south here. The street runs E-W, and the goal lies near Biscayne Blvd just east of me.

Facing ESE (121°): NE 15th St runs east toward the Biscayne intersection (traffic light visible ahead-left). The road surface is clear ahead. Let me click on the asphalt down the road to walk east toward the intersection.
Crosshair is on the asphalt heading east toward the intersection. Let me click (mouse_down + mouse_up) to walk there.

Moved one step east (heading snapped to 89°); distance ~142m (goal is due south, so E-W movement doesn't reduce it yet). I need to reach the Biscayne intersection just ahead to turn south. Let me view. Now facing east (89°) with an intersection ahead.

Clear now: I'm on NE 15th St (~x460); the goal is almost due south (x440, ~170px down) on NE 14th St. Biscayne Blvd (the N-S road) is just east of me at x520 — the intersection right ahead in the pano. Plan: east to Biscayne → turn south → down to NE 14th St → west to goal.

Facing east; the Biscayne intersection is ahead (traffic light). Despite a slight east backtrack, Biscayne (x520) is still the closest southbound road to the goal's longitude. Let me walk east toward the intersection by clicking clear asphalt ahead.
That step moved me 3 panos east (steps=4), and distance rose to 150.6m — I may have passed Biscayne. Let me check the map.

I'm now at the Biscayne/15th intersection (blue "YOU" at x510; green "START" marks where I began). Now I turn south onto Biscayne. Heading is 89° (east); I need ~180° (south), so I'll drag left (increases heading). Let me close the map and rotate.

Heading now 159° (SSE). Let me view to find Biscayne's southbound roadway. This is the Biscayne Blvd intersection — sign reads "NE 15 ST / Alvah Chapman Blvd", Omni Station overhead. Downtown Miami's skyline ahead marks south = Biscayne southbound, exactly my direction.
Crosshair is on Biscayne's southbound roadway toward downtown. Let me click to walk south.

Walking south on Biscayne now (heading 182°), distance dropped 150.6m → 118.3m. Let me view and continue south. Facing south down Biscayne (182°), skyline ahead, cross street visible near the horizon. Distance 118m.

Distance now 103.5m (steps 12). I'm progressing south on Biscayne. Let me view and continue south toward NE 14th St where I'll turn west. Still heading south on Biscayne, cross street ahead. Let me take another moderate step south (cursor is already on clear center asphalt).
Distance 95.3m. Let me open the map to pinpoint where to turn west onto NE 14th St.

Position update: YOU (blue) are now on Biscayne at the NE 14th Terrace level. The GOAL is one more block south (NE 14th St, y≈555) and ~75px west. I'll continue south to the NE 14th St intersection, then turn west. Let me close the map and step south.
On Biscayne heading south, NE 14th St intersection ahead. Let me step south (cursor is on clear center asphalt) and reassess.
Distance 81.7m. Let me view to check if I've reached NE 14th St. Still on Biscayne, intersection ahead. I'm close (81.7m). Let me check the map to precisely locate the NE 14th St turn.
YOU are on Biscayne between 14th Terrace and 14th St; still ~88px south to the NE 14th St intersection, then ~75px west to the goal. Let me close map and continue south.
NE 14th St intersection is ahead near the horizon. Let me take another moderate step south (cursor on center asphalt).

Distance 69.6m. Let me view to check for the NE 14th St intersection. Distance 69.6m — I'm now only ~19m north of the goal's latitude (NE 14th St). The intersection is just ahead. Let me check the map to nail the westward turn precisely.
YOU are still ~45m north of NE 14th St; the goal needs ~47m south then ~51m west. Let me close the map and keep stepping south toward the 14th St intersection.

Distance 59.3m. Let me view. The NE 14th St intersection is clearly ahead (signal gantry). I'll step south to reach it, then turn west toward the goal. Let me position the cursor on open asphalt (left of the car ahead) and step.
Crosshair is on clear asphalt. Let me click to step south toward the intersection.

Distance 55.0m. Let me view — I should be at/near the NE 14th St intersection now. I'm at the NE 14th St intersection. The goal is west (to my right). I need to turn right (west, heading ~270°), so I'll drag left to increase heading from 182°. Let me reposition the cursor right and drag left.

Heading 252.7° (WSW). Let me view to find the westbound NE 14th St roadway. Facing WSW (253°); I can see the "NE 14 ST" sign and the cross street. The goal is ~51m due west. Let me rotate slightly more to face due west (270°) to center the westbound roadway.

Facing due west I see a building across Biscayne, with the "NE 14 ST" sign to my left — so NE 14th St heads west from slightly to my south-left. Let me open the map to pinpoint my exact position relative to the westbound turn.
The zoom re-centered on the fixed map center and lost my position. Let me zoom back to level 18 to see YOU and GOAL together.

At zoom 18: YOU are on Biscayne just north of NE 14th St; GOAL is ~46m west and ~22m south on NE 14th St. The dominant direction is west. Let me close the map and identify the westbound NE 14th St roadway in the pano.

I'm slightly north of the 14th St centerline (building dead ahead across Biscayne, "NE 14 ST" sign to my left). The westbound NE 14th St road is to my front-left/SW. Let me rotate right ~25° to ~245° (WSW) to align with the westbound roadway.

Facing WSW (245°): NE 14th St heads west between the two buildings (the "NE 14 St" sign confirms it, gap at center). Let me check my cursor position and aim it at the westbound roadway. Cursor is at (450,559).
The crosshair landed on a car. NE 14th St recedes west through the gap between the buildings (under the "NE 14 St" sign). Let me aim into that gap, just below the horizon on the roadway, and verify.
Crosshair is on the westbound roadway in the building gap (by the "NE 14 St" sign). Let me click to walk west onto NE 14th St.

Distance is now 0.0m — that click walked me 10 panos west along NE 14th St right onto the goal. Let me submit the guess to end the episode at this location. I'm exactly at the goal (0.0m). Submitting the guess.
